output: Section 1020.3 governs the width and capacity of corridors.
According to Section 1003.3.1, a minimum headroom of 80 inches (2032 mm) is required over any walking surface, including walks, corridors, aisles and passageways.

According to Section 1006.2.1, two exits or exit access doorways from any space shall be provided where the design occupant load or the common path of egress travel distance exceeds the values listed in Table 1006.2.1. The cumulative occupant load from adjacent rooms, areas or spaces shall be determined in accordance with Section 1004.2.

According to Section 1005.3.2, Exception 1, for other than Group H and I-2 occupancies, the capacity factor is **0.15 inch (3.8 mm) per occupant** in buildings equipped throughout with an automatic sprinkler system installed in accordance with Section 903.3.1.1 or 903.3.1.2 and an emergency voice/alarm communication system in accordance with Section 907.5.2.2.
Per Section 1005.3.1, Exception 1, the capacity factor is 0.2 inch (5.1 mm) per occupant (for other than Group H and I-2 occupancies).

According to **Section 1609** (Wind Loads), wind loads are governed by this section. Per **Section 1609.1.1** (Determination of Wind Loads), wind loads on every building or structure shall be determined in accordance with **Chapters 26 to 30 of ASCE 7**.
Under Section 1609.3, the ultimate design wind speed, V(ult), for use in the design of Risk Category II buildings and structures shall be obtained from Figure 1609.3(1).

Not found in context.

While the context (Section 1609.3) explains that the ultimate design wind speed, V, for Risk Category II buildings is obtained from Figure 1609.3(1), it does not specify which figure the wind-borne debris region is based on.

Not found in context.

The context references Section 1610 for lateral soil loads, but it does not provide information about foundation walls, retaining wall design, or where minimum design soil loads are found.

Section 1803 governs geotechnical investigations. Specifically, Section 1803.1 (General) states that geotechnical investigations shall be conducted in accordance with Section 1803.2 and reported in accordance with Section 1803.6.
Section **1804.5** addresses grading and fill placement requirements in flood hazard areas.
Section 3109 governs construction seaward of the Coastal Construction Control Line (CCCL).

According to the context, **Section 110.3** (specifically item **1.1** under the Foundation inspection) requires that "In flood hazard areas, upon placement of the lowest floor, including basement, and prior to further vertical construction, the elevation certification shall be submitted to the authority having jurisdiction."

Not found in context.

While Section R322 is referenced in the context, the context does not describe what R322 addresses (flood-resistant construction or coastal high-hazard areas).

According to the context, **Sec. 62-77** governs variances and appeals, including flood variance requests.

Specifically, pursuant to F.S. § 553.73(5), the Board of Building Adjustments and Appeals hears and decides on requests for appeals and variances from the strict application of the flood-resistant construction requirements of the Florida Building Code (Sec. 62-77). This section does not apply to Section 3109 of the Florida Building Code, Building.

gold_section: Collier County Ordinance 2023-64, amended by Ordinance 2024-40

output: According to Section 202, "means of egress" is defined as a way out of a building or structure to a public way. A means of egress consists of three separate and distinct parts: the exit access, the exit and the exit discharge.
Under the definition of "Means of Egress" in Section 202, the three separate and distinct parts of a means of egress are: the exit access, the exit, and the exit discharge.
Not found in context.

According to Section R311.7.1, stairways shall be not less than 36 inches (914 mm) in clear width at all points above the permitted handrail height and below the required headroom height.

The section also specifies that the clear width at and below the handrail height, including treads and landings, shall be not less than 31½ inches (787 mm) where a handrail is installed on one side and 27 inches (698 mm) where handrails are provided on both sides.

Based on the context:

- **Maximum riser height:** Not more than 7 inches (the fraction rendered as "7 /" appears to be truncated, but the section states "not more than 7 / inches (196 mm)"). Per **Section R311.7.5.1**.

- **Minimum tread depth:** Not less than **10 inches (254 mm)**. Per **Section R311.7.5.2**.
According to Section R311.7.3, a flight of stairs shall not have a vertical rise greater than 12 feet 7 inches (3835 mm) between floor levels or landings.
Not found in context.
Under Section 1005.7.1, doors when fully opened shall not reduce the required width by more than 7 inches (178 mm).
Sec. 22-26 of the Collier County Code of Ordinances adopts the Florida Building Code and sets out the County's local additions to it.
Section 107.3 (specifically, the amendment under Section 107.3.1, based on Section 107.3 of the Florida Building Code).
According to the context, the permit exemptions were established for Collier County based on **Section 105.2.2** of the Florida Building Code (and Section 553.73(4)(a), Florida Statutes).
Ordinance No. 2020-48. (SECTION THREE: REPEAL.)
Sec. 62-80 governs requirements for buildings and structures.
Sec. 62-73 establishes the duties and powers of the county floodplain administrator.
Section 4.02.01 contains the dimensional standards table (Table 2.1) of minimum yard requirements for principal structures in base zoning districts.
According to footnote (e), the total of all side yard setbacks shall equal 20% of the lot width, with a maximum of 50 feet. No side yard shall be less than 10 feet.
Section 4.02.03 sets the specific standards for the location of accessory buildings and structures.

Not found in context.

The provided context is Table 1 (Lot Design Requirements), which covers minimum lot area, minimum lot width, and maximum building coverage — but it does not include front yard setback information for the E zoning district.
The minimum lot area for the Estates (E) zoning district is **98,010 square feet**, per Section 4.02.01.

Within 100 feet of the nesting zone of a beach, it is unlawful to perform these activities without obtaining a construction in sea turtle nesting area permit from the County Manager or designee (Section 3.04.02).
According to the context, mechanical, plumbing and electrical systems, including plumbing fixtures, must be "elevated to or above the base flood elevation plus one (1) foot" (Sec. 62-80).

Based on the context, the electrical and plumbing repair work performed by licensed contractors is exempt from a permit when it does not exceed **$1,500.00** in value of materials and labor (Section 105.2.4, under "Electrical:" item 1 and "Plumbing:" item 1).
According to Section 105.2, drywall repair/replacement is exempt from a building permit when it is not fire-related and covers small areas that **do not exceed two sheets of drywall (64 sq. ft.)**.
Under Section 110.3, the permit holder shall submit the spot survey depicting the building foundation **within 10 calendar days** of the foundation inspection.

According to SECTION TWO: INCORPORATION, AS EXHIBIT "A", OF UPDATED WIND ZONE MAPS, Collier County Ordinance 2023-64 incorporated as Exhibit "A" the updated Wind Zone Maps, consisting of Category 1, Category 2, Category 3 and Category 4, which are hereby adopted.

According to SECTION SIX (Penalties for Violation), the maximum fine for violating the provisions of the Ordinance is **Five Hundred Dollars ($500.00)**, or imprisonment not to exceed sixty (60) days in the County jail, or both, at the court's discretion.

Under Collier County LDC 4.02.03, an accessory building shall not occupy an area greater than five (5) percent of the total lot area in all residential zoning districts, or greater than forty (40) percent of any building envelope, whichever is the lesser—provided the total maximum coverage provision for all principal and accessory buildings is not exceeded.

Based on the context, the swimming pool and/or screen enclosure (one- and two-family) has a setback value of "None" listed in the last column, with "10 feet" preceding it.

However, the context does not clearly label which column represents the rear yard setback or specify waterfront versus non-waterfront lots. The specific rear yard setback value for a non-waterfront lot cannot be definitively determined from the labeled information provided.
